summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Games / criticism, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are The Myst lifts over Call of the Elder Gods, granting it a May release date, Linux kernel maintainers are following through on removing Intel 486 support, and The Moto Pad is Motorola’s first US-bound tablet in over a decade.

mainItems.title: The Myst lifts over Call of the Elder Gods, granting it a May release date
mainItems.summary: Adventure puzzle games inevitably always come back to Myst , and 2020's Call of the Sea was no different.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Linux kernel maintainers are following through on removing Intel 486 support

mainItems.title: The Moto Pad is Motorola’s first US-bound tablet in over a decade
mainItems.summary: Motorola announced two new devices today, including one of its first tablets to be available from a US carrier since Verizon's Xyboard tablets in 2011.

mainItems.title: Return of the Obra Dinn developer Lucas Pope doesn't feel comfortable talking about new projects because maybe they'll be "slurped up by AI"
